Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Data Center revenue surpassed prior records as Blackwell architecture products entered volume production, extending strong demand from hyperscale cloud providers and enterprise customers building artificial intelligence infrastructure beyond the Hopper generation.

Gaming revenue grew 16 percent year-over-year as the product refresh cycle continued, with desktop and laptop attach rates for our latest discrete GPU generation improving relative to prior launches.

Gross margin contracted modestly from peak levels as Blackwell production ramp introduced initial manufacturing costs, partially offset by the prior year expansion from higher-value data center configurations and improved yields on our leading-node process.

Export control restrictions imposed by the United States government on advanced semiconductor shipments to certain countries required us to develop alternative product configurations, reducing revenue opportunity in affected markets.

Operating expenses grew at a slower rate than revenue as scale benefits offset continued research and development investment in next-generation GPU architectures, networking technology, and software platforms for the artificial intelligence ecosystem.

Item 3. Other Information
